uk: 🕃 Передсвяття походу з чесним і животворним Хрестом Господнім
saints.en: Forefeast of the Procession of the Precious and Life-Giving Cross of the Lord. St. and Righteous Eudocimus

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи взиваю» – 6 стихир: 3 передсвяття і 3 прп., Сл.: прп., І нині: передсвяття. Стиховня Октоїха. Слава, І нині: передсвяття. По «Отче наш»: тропар прп., Слава, І нині: передсвяття. Відпуст середній з помин. служби дня і прп.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар передсвяття (2), Слава: прп., І нині: передсвяття. Катизми чергові і сідальні октоїха. Псалом 50. Канон Октоїха, передсвяття і прп. По 9-ій – світильний з Октоїха, також Слава: світильний прп., якщо має, І нині: передсвяття. Стиховня Октоїха, Слава: прп., І нині: передсвяття. Тропар прп., Слава, І нині: передсвяття.
Часи: тропар передсвяття, також на 3-му і 9-му ще Слава: прп., кондаки – поперемінно, тобто: на 1-му і 6-му – передсвяття, на 3-му і 9-му – прп.
Літургія: Тропар і кондак передсвяття. Прокімен, алилуя, причасний – дня. Ап. – 2 Кор. 169 зач.; 1, 12-20. Єв. – Мт. 91 зач.; 22, 23-33.

troparion_en.text: O Lord, save Your people, / and bless Your inheritance! / Grant victories to the Orthodox Christians / over their adversaries. / And by virtue of the Cross, / preserve Your habitation!

kontakion_en.text: As You were voluntarily crucified for our sake, / grant mercy to those who are called by Your name; / make all Orthodox Christians glad by Your power, / granting them victories over their adversaries, / by bestowing on them the invincible trophy, Your weapon of peace!

matched_oca_saint: Forefeast of the Procession of the Honorable and Lifegiving Cross of the Lord

oca_saints.name: Forefeast of the Procession of the Honorable and Lifegiving Cross of the Lord

troparion.text: O Lord, save Your people, / and bless Your inheritance! / Grant victories to the Orthodox Christians / over their adversaries. / And by virtue of the Cross, / preserve Your habitation!

kontakion.text: As You were voluntarily crucified for our sake, / grant mercy to those who are called by Your name; / make all Orthodox Christians glad by Your power, / granting them victories over their adversaries, / by bestowing on them the invincible trophy, Your weapon of peace!

troparion.text: He who called you from earth to heaven, / keeps your body unharmed after death, holy Eudocimus; / for you lived a sober and holy life / and did not defile your flesh; / so with boldness intercede with Christ that we may be saved.

kontakion.text: Today your honored memory has assembled us at the holy shrine of your sacred relics; / all then who approach and venerate them / are rescued from the evil malice of demons / and are swiftly delivered from various diseases, blessed Eudocimus.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Julitta, O Lord, / through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through her intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Julitta;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Holy Bishop Germanus, our protector, honor and consolation of the church of Gaul.  / You gave up glory and riches in order to follow Christ our God with humility.  / You fought against heresies and caused the true Faith to triumph. /  O Father of the Auxerrians, refuge of the unfortunate, pray to Christ to strengthen us in mercy.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Benjamin, Metropolitan of Petrograd and Gdovsk, and those with him

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Arsenius, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

The Noble Joseph, /  when he had taken down Your most pure Body from the tree,  /  wrapped it in fine linen and anointed it with spices, / and placed it in a new tomb.  / But You rose on the third day, O Lord,  / granting the world great mercy.
